column1: The optimized At-Ca NPs (F5) showed a signiﬁcant (p < 0.05) decrease in paw oedema
in all time intervals compared to untreated control, placebo NPs and free At-Ca as demon-
strated in Figure 8. The anti-inﬂammatory effect of both placebo NPs and untreated control
is statistically non-signiﬁcant producing similar results of percent decrease in edema’s
weight. At 6 h time interval, it is obviously noted that free At-Ca has a signiﬁcant reduction
in edema’s weight compared with untreated control and placebo NPs with a non-signiﬁcant
difference in the remaining time intervals till 72 h (Figure 8).
